subject: marathon runners
one_liner: Autonomous perimeter surveillance, real-time contact classification, and quadcopter overwatch for the recreational 10K.
brand_voice_notes: Straight-faced defense-contractor copy applied to a Saturday jog. Procurement-speak, capability briefs, rules of engagement, domain awareness. Never acknowledges the absurdity. The geese are a credible adversary and we treat them as one.

hero.h1: Total domain awareness for the recreational loop.
hero.sub: Picket brings autonomous perimeter surveillance, sub-second contact classification, and quadcopter overwatch to your morning run. The geese have held the initiative long enough.

features.title: Sentry Mesh
features.blurb: Solar surveillance pylons stand themselves up along your usual route and hand off tracking as you pass. Coverage gaps close in under 40 seconds. You will not be surprised by a sprinkler again.

features.title: Contact Classification
features.blurb: Every cyclist, dog walker, and squirrel inside your operating area is tagged friendly, neutral, or hostile and pushed to your watch. Confidence updates twice a second. Most strollers resolve to neutral.

features.title: Overwatch
features.blurb: A company-owned quadcopter holds a pattern above your easy run and relays a top-down feed to the ground station. It does not carry anything. We have been clear with the FAA about this.

pricing_tiers.name: Patrol
pricing_tiers.tagline: For the runner securing a single loop.

pricing_tiers.name: Forward Operating
pricing_tiers.tagline: For the household that has decided the cul-de-sac is contested.

pricing_tiers.name: Theater Command
pricing_tiers.tagline: For municipalities, run clubs, and one very committed retiree.
perks: We do not put this tier in writing

testimonials.quote: I have not been passed on the Greenway since we stood up the mesh. The telemetry is unambiguous.
testimonials.name: Dale Ferro
testimonials.title: Age-Group Podium Finisher (M 50-54), Reston Pace Collective

testimonials.quote: The geese relocated to a different pond within nine days. I did nothing the township could cite.
testimonials.name: Marguerite Voss
testimonials.title: Homeowner, Contested Cul-de-Sac

testimonials.quote: Explaining the procurement to my wife was difficult. Explaining the overwatch feed was not.
testimonials.name: Tomas Brandt
testimonials.title: Recreational Runner, 14-Minute 5K Aspirant

demo_screen_concept: A tactical command console for one morning run. A top-down map of a city-park loop shows the runner as a blue BLUFOR icon mid-route, fixed surveillance pylons as numbered nodes with coverage arcs, and live contacts (an off-leash dog, a gaggle of geese, two cyclists, a rival age-group runner) moving along the paths, each tagged friendly / neutral / hostile with a live confidence percentage. A right-hand panel holds a scrolling threat feed, a Rules of Engagement switch list (maintain 3m standoff from off-leash dogs, do not classify children, yield to ambulances), and current pace. Clicking a contact opens a classification popover to reclassify it; the rival runner carries a DESIGNATE control. ROE toggles flip live and write entries to the feed. A filter shows hostiles only.

design_direction.style: Tactical command & control (C2) interface, defense-grade dark HUD. Olive near-black ground with a faint reticle grid and scanline wash, corner-bracket framing on every panel, monospace telemetry readouts, blinking status LEDs, a classification banner running edge to edge, and condensed stencil-grotesque headers. Radar-green is the system color; alert-red marks hostiles. Reads like a procurement portal for a defense prime that has misunderstood the customer.
design_direction.archetype: Real-Time / Operations Landing (live status hero, capability metrics, how-it-works, demo CTA)
design_direction.motion_vocab: Sweeping radar arc on the hero, blinking status dots, panel corner-brackets that draw in on scroll, contact pings that fade. All motion gated behind prefers-reduced-motion.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Full-bleed dark hero with a top classification banner (UNCLASSIFIED // FOR RECREATIONAL USE), a condensed left-aligned headline over a live mock telemetry strip (pace, contacts tracked, mesh status), a single primary CTA, and a right-side reticle/radar graphic rendered in CSS. Spec ribbon of capability stats below the fold.
